title: Agrostemma flos-cuculi
taxonomy: Agrostemma flos-cuculi is a member of the family Polemoniaceae, commonly known as the Jacob's-ladder or Greek valerian family. This family consists of about 15 genera and 25 species, mostly native to Europe and North America. The genus Agrostemma flos-cuculi is a monotypic genus, containing only one species, the Corn Cockle. It is also sometimes classified in the family Caryophyllaceae. The classification may vary depending on the source, but generally, it is placed in the family Polemoniaceae.
uses: Agrostemma flos-cuculi, also known as the corn cockle, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's extracts have been found to possess antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help combat infections. In traditional European medicine, Agrostemma flos-cuculi was used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. However, it's essential to note that the plant's medicinal uses have not been extensively scientifically studied, and more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Nonetheless, some studies suggest that the plant's compounds may have potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

side_effects: Agrostemma flos-cuculi, also known as corn cockle, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
